name: Mesopotamian Arabic

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Gilit Mesopotamian Arabic (meso1252).

notes: Skeleton. Gelet (gilit) Mesopotamian Arabic, a Semitic (Afroasiatic) variety written in the Arabic abjad. Consonant letters are mapped to IPA; the reflex of qāf ⟨ق⟩ is the voiced velar [ɡ] characteristic of gilit dialects, jīm ⟨ج⟩ is [dʒ], and the interdentals ⟨ث ذ ظ⟩ are retained. Short vowels are not written in the abjad and, apart from the optional harakāt, are left unencoded; gemination (shadda) and stress are likewise not recovered, so a high PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Gilit Mesopotamian Arabic (meso1252)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
